author: Quoia75
msg:
**Is anyone up for a discussion on how to manage comms with ULOs today?**


Just read in the old TG that there will be **no dedicated Discord or Telegram channel for ULOs**. That means UNOs will be primarily responsible for providing ULO support.


Assuming all (or most) UNOs here have other primary jobs, what ways are you exploring to effectively manage communication with your license operators?


As the primary owner/operator of another business, I’m considering hiring a part-time community manager to run a Discord server. I’m also considering setting aside a block of hours each week where I’m personally available to answer questions.


If questions pop up that I don’t have a timely answer to, I’ll reach out directly to Unity support and also run a search here in the official UNO TG to see if the problem already has a solution.


**What will you be doing?**

author: Fred Dalit
msg: [File: AnimatedSticker.tgs]

author: Calus B
msg: They will also be a communication option inside the app

author: Quoia75
msg:
Aware - and that's necessary I think, but if you are managing hundreds (or thousands) of licenses, that can quickly get crazy.

When the API becomes available I'm going to explore having questions routed to a Discord server for easier management. 👌

author: Sören Lewis Müller
msg:
I currently do everything via email. But of course you can invite them to a TG or WhatsApp Group Chat OR Channel. 
I personally like the idea of a channel for universal info & then in-app or DM/Email for 1on1 convo.

And don't wan't a group thing as one bad apple could do too much damage😁

author: Quoia75
msg:
So you are a prime example of what it looks like to already have a good amount of license distributed. (Congrats BTW!)

Would you say the amount of support questions you are getting warrants hiring help to manage? Or have you found a cadence with templates etc. that fits you? How many days a week, how many hours per day does managing questions demand of you?

For quality of life it's incumbent upon me to find a good equilibrium between providing good quality support but not being chained to  a desk or device all day, every day.

Took me hours each days for about 3-4 weeks to find solid enough ULOs to fill a node. And whole weekends.. Some people had 100 questions😁 Answered them all at start, then took it a bit easy..

Don't wanna spam this chat but if you have many nodes it's only wise to find someone to manage it all for you. Only way it becomes really passive.

And you could also use this person to market it not just for support.

author: Quoia75
msg:
**Another question for any of you who have hired community managers** (I have not but as stated, exploring):


Just from discussions with other Web 3 contractors, I notice that people hired for that role prefer less traditional communication. So for example, I’m use to having bi-monthly video calls with my sub-contractors for content and web development stuff. 


People hired for community management roles tend to prefer pure messaging or email comms (?)


Does that track with anyone else?


Just making sure this older Web 2 dev is keeping up with what’s best for the Web 3 workforce.

author: Quoia75
msg: I guess anyone from Defiant could also answer that - how do you all prefer to have communications managed with your project manager or hiring party?

Hello All, I've transferred a Unity NFT to an other wallet. There it showed up (after import). Because of the connections errors I got (I thought they were correlated with this transfer) I transferred the NFT back to the original wallet. Now that the connection problems are over I transferred the NFT again. This time I can't import it into MetaMask. I get every time the message: NFT can’t be added as the ownership details do not match. Make sure you have entered correct information. 
I'm lost can maybe someone give me a hint in the right direction? Thanks!
